content:
# elizaos/eliza Daily Update (May 2, 2026)

## OVERVIEW 
Development on May 2, 2026, focused on stabilizing the agent runtime across diverse environments. Key achievements include resolving critical segfaults on headless Linux, fixing Telegram bot integration races, and correcting authentication logic for Codex CLI. The team also improved CI reliability for Windows Server 2025 and updated core dependencies to ensure framework modularity and performance.

## KEY TECHNICAL DEVELOPMENTS

**Runtime Stability & Environment Compatibility**
*   Resolved a process-level segfault on headless Linux servers by implementing a check to skip the OS keychain when D-Bus is unavailable ([#7230](https://github.com/elizaos/eliza/pull/7230)).
*   Improved CI reliability by handling non-zero exit codes from Inno Setup on Windows Server 2025 runners ([#7232](https://github.com/elizaos/eliza/pull/7232)).

**Dependency & Tooling Updates**
*   Updated `viem` to v2.48.8 ([#7237](https://github.com/elizaos/eliza/pull/7237)).
*   Updated `tsdown` to ^0.21.0 ([#7236](https://github.com/elizaos/eliza/pull/7236)).
*   Upgraded Node.js environment to v24 ([#7239](https://github.com/elizaos/eliza/pull/7239)).

## NEWLY OPENED PULL REQUESTS
*   None.

## CLOSED ISSUES

**Authentication & Configuration Fixes**
*   Resolved issues with Codex CLI authentication by adding support for modern `tokens.access_token` shapes and ensuring proper fallback to `OPENAI_API_KEY` ([#7238](https://github.com/elizaos/eliza/issues/7238), [#7243](https://github.com/elizaos/eliza/issues/7243)).
*   Fixed Telegram bot token bridging, ensuring configuration from the dashboard correctly propagates to the runtime environment ([#7240](https://github.com/elizaos/eliza/issues/7240)).

**Architecture & Runtime Reliability**
*   Corrected `MiladyClient` class duplication, ensuring domain method augmentations are correctly applied to the canonical client instance ([#7233](https://github.com/elizaos/eliza/issues/7233), [#7244](https://github.com/elizaos/eliza/issues/7244)).
*   Resolved Telegram bot race conditions and Bun-specific `readonly` property errors via policy gating and patching Telegraf ([#7241](https://github.com/elizaos/eliza/issues/7241), [#7245](https://github.com/elizaos/eliza/issues/7245)).
*   Addressed headless Linux runtime crashes caused by `@napi-rs/keyring` segfaults ([#7231](https://github.com/elizaos/eliza/issues/7231)).

## NEW ISSUES

**Runtime & Integration Challenges**
*   [#7231](https://github.com/elizaos/eliza/issues/7231): Agent runtime crashes on headless Linux due to D-Bus Secret Service unavailability.
*   [#7233](https://github.com/elizaos/eliza/issues/7233): Duplicate `MiladyClient` class definitions causing method augmentation failures.
*   [#7238](https://github.com/elizaos/eliza/issues/7238): Incompatibility with modern Codex CLI `auth.json` structures.
*   [#7240](https://github.com/elizaos/eliza/issues/7240): Telegram bot token configuration not bridging from dashboard to plugin.
*   [#7241](https://github.com/elizaos/eliza/issues/7241): Telegraf `bot.launch()` errors under Bun due to readonly property assignment.
*   [#7243](https://github.com/elizaos/eliza/issues/7243): Authentication failure reporting 'install required' due to legacy credential shape checks.
*   [#7244](https://github.com/elizaos/eliza/issues/7244): Prototype splitting in `MiladyClient` leading to undefined methods.
*   [#7245](https://github.com/elizaos/eliza/issues/7245): Message loss caused by concurrent Telegram polling between the wrapper and the plugin.

## ACTIVE ISSUES
*   None.
